title: Python Decorators
summary: A short explanation of what this topic covers.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to return the wrapper function
practice_idea: Create a decorator that logs function calls.

title: Object-Oriented Programming: Inheritance
summary: Learn how subclasses inherit attributes and methods from base classes to write modular and reusable code.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to call super().__init__() in the subclass constructor
Using inheritance when object composition would be more appropriate
practice_idea: Create a base class 'Vehicle' and a subclass 'ElectricCar' that overrides a 'fuel_type' method and extends the parent constructor with a 'battery_capacity' attribute.

title: Python List Comprehensions
summary: Master the syntax of list comprehensions to write concise, readable, and fast loops.
common_mistakes: Writing overly complex one-liners that harm readability
Forgetting the correct order of clauses in nested loops
practice_idea: Take a standard nested loop that filters even numbers and squares them, and refactor it into a clean, single-line list comprehension.

title: Python Context Managers
summary: Understand how the 'with' statement manages resources safely and how to build your own context managers.
common_mistakes: Forgetting to handle exceptions inside the __exit__ method
Attempting to use a resource after the context block has closed
practice_idea: Create a custom context manager using a class or the @contextmanager decorator that measures and prints the execution time of the code block inside it.

title: Python Generators
summary: Learn how to use generator functions and the yield keyword to create memory-efficient iterators.
common_mistakes: Using return instead of yield to produce multiple values
Trying to reuse an exhausted generator
practice_idea: Write a generator function that streams a massive text file line-by-line without loading the entire file into RAM.
